name: Keiki and the Plow
tagline: U-pick veggies, mud kitchens, goats, and a pirate ship — a rare working farm open to families.
parking: Small lot at 587 Pakala St, Hawaii Kai
bestTime: Arrive right at 9am — it fills up fast and the 2-hour window goes quickly
talkStory: Founded by a local family in Hawaii Kai's suburban foothills, Keiki and the Plow is a community-supported urban farm open to families for just two hours each Saturday morning. The pirate ship play structure was built by hand from reclaimed lumber. Kids harvest real vegetables from the rows and the mud kitchen encourages messy creative play. It is one of the few working farms in east Honolulu and a grassroots gem most tourists never find.
